meta.analyst_note: All signals sourced from verified public disclosures, financial filings, and credible news outlets. No hallucinated data.

confidence_key.A: Primary source: company filing, regulator, exchange filing, shareholder letter
confidence_key.B: Credible news outlet citing primary source or analyst note
confidence_key.C: Industry report, estimate, or secondary analysis
confidence_key.D: Social media claim or unverified assertion

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit's NOV of Rs 9,203 crore surpassed Zomato's food delivery NOV for the first time, marking quick commerce as Eternal's largest vertical by order value. NOV grew 127% YoY, while Blinkit adjusted revenue reached Rs 2,400 crore.
signals.source_name: Medianama / Eternal Q1 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update Blinkit quarterly NOV series; flag quick-commerce overtaking food delivery as inflection point for category investment.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: NOV YoY growth partly inflated by comparison with a slower Q1 FY25 base; adjusted revenue of Rs 2,400 crore includes 1P inventory model impact from FY26.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit's average monthly transacting customers rose to 16.9 million in Q1 FY26 from 7.6 million in Q1 FY25, more than doubling year-on-year.
signals.source_name: Medianama / Eternal Q1 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update Blinkit user growth series; compare with Instamart MTU trajectory for market sizing.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: MTVU definition may differ from Swiggy Instamart's reporting definition; direct comparison requires definitional alignment.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit reported 137% YoY NOV growth (highest in 10 quarters), added 272 net new stores to reach 1,816, and moved 80% of business to inventory ownership model. EBITDA loss stood at Rs 156 crore with -1.3% margin (improved from -1.8% in Q1).
signals.source_name: Angel One / Eternal Q2 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update Blinkit quarterly EBITDA and NOV series; note margin improvement trajectory for profitability forecasting.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: EBITDA loss widened YoY (from Rs 8 crore loss YoY) due to aggressive store expansion and 1P model transition; attributed to investment phase. Revenue comparisons distorted by accounting change.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit added 272 net new dark stores in Q2 FY26, taking total to 1,816. The 1P inventory model now covers 80% of business, driving revenue accounting shift.
signals.source_name: LinkedIn / Startup Pedia citing Eternal Q2 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update dark store count series for Blinkit; recalculate per-store throughput benchmark for city density planning.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Net new stores includes replacements/relocations; gross additions are higher. 1P model shift makes pre-FY26 revenue comparisons unreliable.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit turned adjusted EBITDA positive for the first time at Rs 4 crore, a dramatic improvement from a Rs 103 crore loss in the year-ago quarter and Rs 156 crore loss in Q2 FY26. NOV reached Rs 13,300 crore with 121% YoY growth (like-for-like 130%+). Revenue surged to Rs 12,256 crore (776% YoY, inflated by 1P accounting).
signals.source_name: Economic Times / BW Disrupt / Eternal Q3 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Flag EBITDA breakeven milestone in Executive Decision Brief; update EBITDA margin trajectory for FY27 projection.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Rs 4 crore positive EBITDA is symbolic but very thin (0.03% of NOV margin). Sustainability depends on store maturity curve and competitive intensity. Revenue figure of Rs 12,256 crore includes full 1P inventory value.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit reached 2,027 dark stores by end of Q3 FY26, adding ~211 net new stores during the quarter. Company targets 3,000 stores by March 2027.
signals.source_name: LinkedIn / iCoderz citing Eternal Q3 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update dark store trajectory; model store density vs. throughput for tier-2/3 city entry planning.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Store count figure is from secondary sources; cross-referenced with Q4 reported store delta of 216 stores from 2,027 to 2,243 confirms accuracy.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit NOV stood at Rs 13,300 crore in Q3 FY26, more than doubling from Rs 6,020 crore in Q3 FY25. Like-for-like NOV growth was 130%+ YoY. Adjusted revenue was Rs 12,256 crore, contributing 75% of Eternal's consolidated revenue.
signals.source_name: Storyboard18 / Eternal Q3 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update NOV quarterly series; annotate 1P accounting change impact starting FY26 for any like-for-like comparisons.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: NOV YoY growth partly reflects 1P model shift; like-for-like growth of 130%+ is the more meaningful comparison. Revenue includes full goods value under 1P model.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit reported NOV of Rs 14,386 crore in Q4 FY26, up 95.4% YoY and 8.2% QoQ. Revenue rose to Rs 13,232 crore (from Rs 1,709 crore YoY, inflated by 1P accounting). Like-for-like quick commerce revenue grew 126% YoY.
signals.source_name: Outlook Business / Indian Startup News / Eternal Q4 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update NOV quarterly series; use like-for-like 126% growth for trend analysis rather than reported 675% reported revenue growth.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Reported revenue YoY growth (675%) is misleading due to 1P model change. Like-for-like growth of 126% YoY is the correct comparable metric.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit reported adjusted EBITDA of Rs 37 crore in Q4 FY26, marking a decisive improvement from Rs 4 crore in Q3 FY26 and a loss of Rs 178 crore in Q4 FY25. Adjusted EBITDA margin stood at 0.3% of NOV. This is the second consecutive quarter of positive EBITDA and confirms the profitability trajectory.
signals.source_name: Indian Startup News / Outlook Business / Eternal Q4 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update EBITDA trajectory chart in Benchmark Cockpit; flag Blinkit EBITDA-positive as key strategic milestone in Evidence Ledger.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: 0.3% EBITDA margin is still very thin; company has guided to 5-6% EBITDA margin target long-term. EBITDA profitability may fluctuate with aggressive store expansion.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit added 216 net new dark stores in Q4 FY26, taking total store count to 2,243 (up from 1,301 a year ago). Average monthly transacting customers reached 27.2 million. Orders reached 273.9 million during the quarter. Average throughput of ~2,284 orders/store/month reported.
signals.source_name: LinkedIn / Indian Startup News / Eternal Q4 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update dark store count and per-store metrics in Benchmark Cockpit; model city-level store density thresholds for hyperlocal launch decisions.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Net store additions may include replacements. Throughput per store figure (~2,284/month) is an average; mature stores likely significantly higher, new stores dragging average down.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit processed 273.9 million orders in Q4 FY26 with 27.2 million average monthly transacting customers and 409,000 average monthly active delivery partners. Quick commerce NOV grew 95.4% YoY.
signals.source_name: Indian Startup News / Eternal Q4 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update order volume, MTU, and delivery partner series for Blinkit. Calculate implied AOV (NOV/orders) for cross-platform comparison with Instamart.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: AOV implied at ~Rs 525. Order and user counts may include some double-counting across platforms (Zomato+Blinkit overlap).

signals.signal_summary: Eternal (Zomato parent) reported consolidated PAT of Rs 174 crore (346% YoY), revenue from operations of Rs 17,292 crore (196% YoY), and B2C NOV of Rs 26,880 crore (54% YoY). Adjusted EBITDA of Rs 429 crore (160% YoY).
signals.source_name: Economic Times / Business Standard / Eternal Q4 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update consolidated Eternal KPIs for cross-vertical benchmarking; use for total market sizing.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Consolidated figures include food delivery, quick commerce, Hyperpure, and District. Revenue YoY inflated by 1P accounting change in Blinkit.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit CEO Albinder Dhindsa announced plan to reach 3,000 dark stores by March 2027, up from 2,027 at end of Q3 FY26. If competition moderates, the company could target 3,500-4,000 stores. Focus remains on top 8-10 cities for density before tier expansion.
signals.source_name: Economic Times / Business Standard / CIOL
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Model city-level store expansion timeline for hyperlocal launch sequencing; use 3,000 target for FY27 capacity planning.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Target is forward-looking guidance, not a commitment. Execution depends on real estate availability, regulatory clearances, and competitive intensity.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit held approximately 48% market share in India's quick commerce sector in 2025, per Reuters citing industry data. Swiggy Instamart held 24% and Zepto 22%. BofA Research cited Blinkit at over 50% market share.
signals.source_name: Reuters / WebbyTemplate / Economic Times citing BofA
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update market share estimates for Blinkit vs. Instamart vs. Zepto in Benchmark Cockpit. Use 48-50% as Blinkit range.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Market share estimates vary by source (48% vs >50%). Based on GOV share; may differ by city or category. No single authoritative regulator publishes this data.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit's retail media revenue now exceeds Rs 1,000 crore, with claims of 1.5-2x better returns than Meta or Google for D2C brands. D2C brands are shifting 60-70% of their ad budgets to quick-commerce platforms.
signals.source_name: LinkedIn (Naveen Reddy Guntaka) citing industry data
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Flag Blinkit retail media as high-priority campaign channel in Opportunity Engine; model ROAS benchmarks vs. Meta/Google for brand pitches.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Rs 1,000 crore figure is from secondary LinkedIn sources, not from Eternal's official financial disclosures. The 1.5-2x return vs. Meta/Google claim is anecdotal and needs brand-specific validation. Eternal does not break out retail media revenue separately in filings.

signals.signal_summary: Quick commerce platforms (Blinkit, Instamart, Zepto) are expected to collectively clock Rs 4,900 crore in ad revenue in FY26, up from Rs 3,000 crore. This is part of a broader e-commerce ad revenue trend reaching Rs 28,000 crore by 2026 per Deloitte.
signals.source_name: BrandEquity (Economic Times) / LinkedIn / Deloitte
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Use Rs 4,900 crore Q-commerce ad pool as total addressable market for retail media module; estimate Blinkit's share at ~40-50% (~Rs 2,000-2,500 crore) given market position.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Collective figure for all Q-commerce platforms; Blinkit's individual share is not separately disclosed. Deloitte's methodology for the Rs 28,000 crore e-commerce ad revenue figure is not detailed in the source.

signals.signal_summary: S&P Global projects Eternal's FY26 revenue at Rs 568 billion (163% YoY growth) with quick commerce (Blinkit) revenue surging 650% to Rs 391 billion, nearing 70% of total sales.
signals.source_name: S&P Global Market Intelligence
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Use S&P FY26 projection as benchmark for annual forecasting; compare against actual quarterly disclosures to track accuracy.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Analyst projection as of Jan 2026; 650% revenue surge includes 1P accounting change impact. Like-for-like growth will be significantly lower.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit shifted to an inventory-led (1P) model from Q1 FY26. By Q3 FY26, ~90% of Blinkit's NOV comes from own inventory. This change means reported revenue now includes full value of goods sold, not just commissions, inflating YoY revenue comparisons significantly.
signals.source_name: Rachana Ranade / Eternal Q3 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Add annotation to all FY26 Blinkit revenue figures noting 1P model change. Use NOV and like-for-like growth as primary KPIs instead of reported revenue.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Revenue comparability with pre-FY26 periods is broken. NOV remains the apples-to-apples metric. Gross margin structure changes under 1P model.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit CEO Albinder Dhindsa stated the company aims to expand into tier-II and tier-III cities while maintaining density in top cities. Eternal CFO Akshant Goyal indicated Blinkit is experiencing growth in smaller cities and will expand presence gradually.
signals.source_name: Business Standard / Medial App
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Identify tier-2/3 cities on Blinkit's expansion radar; prioritize these for hyperlocal campaign planning and early-mover brand listings.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: No specific city list disclosed. Tier-2 expansion pace depends on unit economics and competitive dynamics. Store targets are CEO guidance, not commitments.

signals.signal_summary: Eternal management guided for Blinkit's adjusted EBITDA margin to expand to 5-6% of NOV over the medium term. If competition moderates, the company could target 3,500-4,000 stores, though this would keep the pace of NOV growth above 100% YoY.
signals.source_name: BW Disrupt / Eternal Q3 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Model EBITDA margin trajectory from current 0.3% to 5-6% target for investment timing decisions. Use as input for long-term category profitability models.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Management guidance, not a commitment. Timeline to 5-6% EBITDA margin not specified. Competitive intensity and expansion pace could delay achievement.

signals.signal_summary: Eternal expects to double its annual net order value to $20 billion in less than two years, led by Blinkit's quick commerce growth. The company took 18 years to reach $10 billion in annual NOV across all segments.
signals.source_name: Times of India
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Use $20B NOV target as top-line market sizing input for hyperlocal opportunity models.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Forward-looking company guidance. Includes food delivery, quick commerce, and District NOV. Exchange rate assumptions not specified.

signals.signal_summary: Blinkit and Zomato delivered a record-breaking 7.5 million orders on New Year's Eve 2025, with over 450,000 delivery partners serving 6.3 million customers. Over 5,200 orders per minute at peak.
signals.source_name: Entrepreneur Loop
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Use peak-day order data for demand surge planning and campaign timing during festive periods.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Combined Zomato + Blinkit figure; individual platform breakdown not disclosed. Peak data is for a single day and not representative of typical throughput.

signals.signal_summary: Zomato's food delivery NOV stood at Rs 9,757 crore in Q4 FY26, up 18.8% YoY. Adjusted revenue rose 29.7% YoY to Rs 3,125 crore. Adjusted EBITDA was Rs 532 crore with 5.5% margin of NOV, up from Rs 428 crore YoY.
signals.source_name: Indian Startup News / Eternal Q4 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Update food delivery vertical metrics for Zomato; useful for food vs. grocery category sizing.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Food delivery growth of 18.8% YoY is significantly slower than quick commerce's 95.4% YoY, highlighting the structural shift in consumer behavior.

signals.signal_summary: Indians ordered Rs 64,000 crore of goods from quick-commerce platforms in FY25, more than double from the previous fiscal year, per CareEdge. The market is projected to nearly triple by FY28.
signals.source_name: CareEdge / LiveMint
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Use Rs 64,000 crore as FY25 total addressable market (TAM) for Q-commerce. Project FY26/FY27 using 95%+ growth rate from Blinkit as market proxy.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: CareEdge estimate covering top 3 players. Exact definition of what's included in 'quick commerce' (grocery, electronics, pharmacy, etc.) may vary.

signals.signal_summary: Eternal's 'Going-out' business (District) reported NOV of Rs 2,736 crore in Q4 FY26, up 46.5% YoY and 5.8% QoQ. This represents the events/dining-out vertical within the Eternal ecosystem.
signals.source_name: Indian Startup News / Eternal Q4 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Track District NOV as a proxy for out-of-home consumption trends that correlate with hyperlocal demand patterns.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: District is a nascent vertical; 46.5% YoY growth may include base effects. Limited category-level breakouts available.

signals.signal_summary: Hyperpure (Eternal's B2B restaurant supply business) reported revenue growth of 37% YoY in Q4 FY26, improving from 33% in Q3 FY26. The segment turned EBITDA positive, indicating path to sustainable B2B margins.
signals.source_name: Outlook Business / Eternal Q4 FY26 Earnings
signals.recommended_dashboard_action: Track Hyperpure as B2B supply chain proxy; relevant for restaurant-focused hyperlocal campaigns.
signals.limitation_or_caveat: Hyperpure is a B2B vertical; limited direct relevance to B2C hyperlocal marketing but useful for supply-side understanding.

blinkit_nov.source: Eternal Q2 FY26 Earnings (NOV not separately disclosed in sources found)

estimated_blinkit_retail_media_revenue.caveat: Not separately disclosed in Eternal financial filings. Figure is from secondary industry sources.

claimed_roas_vs_meta_google.caveat: Anecdotal claim from secondary sources; no disclosed methodology or independent verification.

contradictions_and_notes.note: A Univest article (April 13, 2026) claimed Blinkit's EBITDA margin turned negative at -3% in Q4 FY26 with GOV of Rs 4,850 crore. This CONTRADICTS the officially reported figures (Rs 14,386 crore NOV, Rs 37 crore positive EBITDA, 0.3% margin). The Univest article was published before official results (April 28, 2026) and appears to contain either pre-result estimates that proved incorrect or different accounting metrics.
contradictions_and_notes.official_reported: NOV Rs 14,386 crore; Adjusted EBITDA Rs 37 crore (+0.3% margin)

contradictions_and_notes.note: From Q1 FY26 (April-June 2025), Blinkit shifted to an inventory-led (1P) model. Revenue now includes full value of goods sold, not just commissions. This makes pre-FY26 vs FY26+ revenue comparisons misleading. NOV (Net Order Value) is the correct comparable metric across periods.
contradictions_and_notes.impact: Reported revenue growth of 675%+ YoY in FY26 is largely accounting-driven; like-for-like growth is 126% for Q4 FY26.
